visualSignals: Lens diagrams with object and image

decisionTree.question: Is the lens converging or diverging?
decisionTree.ifNo: Check lens shape or given focal length

decisionTree.question: Is the image real or virtual?
decisionTree.ifYes: Real: d_i > 0 (opposite side from object); Virtual: d_i < 0 (same side)
decisionTree.ifNo: Calculate d_i and determine from sign

steps.instruction: Identify given quantities: d_o (object distance), f (focal length), or d_i
steps.pitfall: Converging lens: f > 0; Diverging lens: f < 0
steps.check: Object distance d_o is always positive for real objects

steps.pitfall: Don't forget to take reciprocal at the end for d_i
steps.check: Units: all distances in same units (typically cm or m)

steps.pitfall: Sign matters: m > 0 upright, m < 0 inverted; |m| > 1 enlarged, |m| < 1 reduced

steps.instruction: Interpret results: d_i > 0 real image, d_i < 0 virtual image
steps.pitfall: Diverging lenses always form virtual images (d_i < 0)
steps.check: Converging lens: real image if d_o > f, virtual if d_o < f

commonErrors.fix: Real image: d_i > 0 (opposite side from object). Virtual image: d_i < 0 (same side as object).
commonErrors.example: Converging lens with object inside focal length: virtual image on same side, d_i < 0

commonErrors.diagnosis: Forgot negative sign in formula or misinterpreted
commonErrors.fix: m = -d_i/d_o. Negative m means inverted; positive m means upright.
commonErrors.example: Real image from converging lens: d_i > 0, so m < 0 (inverted)

commonErrors.diagnosis: Fundamental error - diverging lenses cannot form real images of real objects
commonErrors.fix: Diverging lens (f < 0) always forms virtual, upright, reduced images for real objects.
commonErrors.example: For diverging lens: d_i is always negative, m is always positive and |m| < 1

workedExamples.problem: An object is placed 30 cm from a converging lens with focal length 10 cm. Find the image distance and magnification.
workedExamples.keyInsight: Object outside 2f (d_o > 2f) gives real, inverted, reduced image between f and 2f

workedExamples.problem: A diverging lens has f = -20 cm. Where is the image of an object placed 30 cm from the lens?

solution.explanation: Given values; f < 0 for diverging lens
Image is virtual (negative d_i) on same side as object
Virtual image is upright and smaller than object

workedExamples.keyInsight: Diverging lenses always produce virtual, upright, reduced images

questions.integral: A converging lens with object inside the focal length produces:
questions.explanation: Object inside f: virtual, upright, enlarged image (magnifying glass)

questions.integral: The focal length of a diverging lens is:
questions.explanation: By convention, f < 0 for diverging lenses, f > 0 for converging

problems.problem: Find image position for object at 15 cm from converging lens with f = 10 cm.
A two-lens system: converging lens (f=20cm) followed by diverging lens (f=-15cm) separated by 50cm. Object placed 30cm before first lens. Find final image.

cheatSheet.quickCheck: Real image: d_i > 0; Virtual: d_i < 0; Inverted: m < 0; Upright: m > 0

masteryCriteria.recognitionSpeed: Identify lens type and expected image characteristics quickly
masteryCriteria.executionSpeed: Solve single lens problem in under 4 minutes
selfCheckQuestions: Can I apply the sign conventions correctly?
Do I know what image type each lens produces?
Can I interpret magnification sign and magnitude?
